meta.title: Quartermaster - Fluent WordPress Query Builder for WP_Query
meta.description: Quartermaster is a fluent, args-first WordPress query builder for WP_Query and WP_Term_Query. Build readable meta_query, tax_query and date_query arguments without an ORM, SQL layer, or lock-in.

hero.badge: ⚓ composer require · PHP 8.3+ · zero runtime dependencies · works in any theme or plugin
hero.title: The fluent WordPress query builder — real WP_Query args out
hero.lede: Chainable, args-first methods for **WP_Query** and **WP_Term_Query**. Sprawling `meta_query` arrays become readable, composable steps — no ORM, no SQL layer, no hidden defaults.

compare.intro: Quartermaster does not replace WordPress queries. It makes the argument-building layer explicit, composable and easier to review.

panels.eyebrow: Raw WP_Query — four things must line up silently

panels.note: Timezone-aware dates; `meta_key` and `orderby` set together, never silently mismatched. The output is the same plain args array.

why.titleRest: Not an ORM. Not a SQL builder. On purpose.
intro: Most WordPress query builders either wrap `$wpdb` in a SQL layer or bolt an Eloquent-style ORM onto WordPress. Quartermaster does neither: every fluent method maps to a real `WP_Query` arg, so caching, plugins, and `pre_get_posts` all keep working.

cards.title: ✨ Readability
cards.body: Query intent expressed step-by-step — no more scanning nested arrays to work out what a query does.

cards.title: 🧩 Composability
cards.body: Add or remove clauses with `when()` / `unless()` without rewriting a large array.

cards.title: 🛡️ Safety
cards.body: Methods are explicit about which WP args they set. Zero side effects by default — `posts()->toArgs()` is an empty array.

cards.title: 🔍 Transparency
cards.body: Nothing runs until you say so — inspect the exact args first, so code review sees the query, not a guess.

features.title: Everyday queries, readable at a glance
features.body: Start simple: seed the post type, chain what you need, call get(). Every method maps to the WP_Query arg you already know — nothing new to learn, just less to mistype.

features.title: See exactly what you built
features.body: toArgs() returns the exact args array before anything runs; explain() adds the applied calls and warnings. Perfect for code review, debugging, and keeping junior crew out of trouble.

tabs.label: introspection

features.title: meta_query without the nesting
features.body: The arrays WordPress developers dread most — meta_query and tax_query — become one call per clause. AND by default, orWhereMeta() and orWhereTax() for OR.

features.title: WP_Term_Query, same fluent treatment
features.body: terms() covers the taxonomy side — hierarchy helpers like childOf(), childless() and excludeTree() included — with the same toArgs() introspection.

features.title: Filter UIs without the URL-parsing slog
features.body: Declaratively map URL query vars to query clauses — pagination, taxonomy filters, numeric meta ranges, search. Nothing reads a query var unless you explicitly bind it.

tabs.label: query-var binding

features.title: Branch without breaking the chain
features.body: when(), unless() and tap() keep branching queries readable — no magic, no hidden state, and every call recorded in explain(). Project-specific sugar goes in opt-in macros.

tabs.label: conditional chains

features.title: Performance flags, spelled out
features.body: idsOnly(), noFoundRows() and cache flags make WordPress's most-forgotten query optimisations first-class, readable calls. Meta queries go further than raw arrays comfortably allow — whereMetaLikeAny(), whereMetaExists(), orWhereMeta() — each mapping to real clauses you can inspect.

tabs.label: lean queries

features.title: Plays well with pre_get_posts
features.body: applyTo() modifies an existing query safely inside pre_get_posts — clause arrays merge with existing ones rather than overwriting, so multiple hooks compose safely. An optional timber() terminal returns Timber objects when Timber is present — runtime-guarded, never hard-coupled.

tabs.label: pre_get_posts

philosophy.title: You decide what goes aboard. Nothing gets smuggled in.
body: Quartermaster is intentionally light-touch: no global state, no ORM, no mutation of WordPress internals and no SQL abstraction. It helps you build the same arguments WordPress already understands.
Sometimes raw WP_Query is fine. Quartermaster earns its keep when queries branch, grow, need tests, or must be safely combined across controller and hook boundaries.

ecosystem.title: Standalone package. Part of the PressGang ecosystem.
ecosystem.intro: Quartermaster works in any WordPress theme or plugin. It also fits naturally with PressGang, where controllers own context and Quartermaster owns the query-argument layer.

items.title: The Loop
items.body: Fetching and rendering entangled in template tags and global state.

items.title: Timber + Twig
items.body: Views separate from PHP, while query construction remains hand-written.

items.title: PressGang
items.body: Controllers give context-building a home and route templates by convention.

items.title: Quartermaster
items.body: Declarative, inspectable query args that keep WordPress machinery untouched.

footer.summary: A fluent, args-first WordPress query builder. Open source, MIT licensed, and part of the pressgang-wp toolkit.
